LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF EVAN NELSON'S MOTION FOR LIMITED EXEMPTION FROM AUTOMATIC STAY IS GRANTED. (CCP 916.) A LIMITED EXEMPTION TO HEAR DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE, WHICH WAS ORIGINALLY TAKEN OFF CALENDAR IN LIGHT OF THE AUTOMATIC STAY, WILL NOT AFFECT THE EFFECTIVENESS OF THE APPEAL PURSUANT TO THE FACTORS ENUMERATED IN VARIAN MEDICAL SYSTEMS, INC. V. DELFINO (2005) 35 CAL.4TH 180, 188-190. DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE IS RE-SET FOR HEARING ON MAY 16, 2014 AT 9:30AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. DEFENDANT SHALL DELIVER AND COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS FILED ON JANUARY 10, 2014 TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN APRIL 11, 2014 AT 4 PM. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). DEFENDANT SHALL SERVE BY MAIL THESE SAME MOVING PAPERS ON PLAINTIFF NO LATER THAN THIS SAME DATE. OPPOSITION AND REPLY SHALL BE FILED AND SERVED IN ACCORDANCE WITH CCP 1005(B). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT TUCKER ELLIS, LLP'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY STATE COURT PROCEEDINGS - THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. THE AGREEMENT IS PROCEDURALLY UNCONSCIONABLE BECAUSE IT WAS PRESENTED TO PLAINTIFF IN A TAKE-IT-OR-LEAVE-IT MANNER AND DEFENDANT FAILED TO PROVIDE A COPY OF THE RULES THAT GOVERN THE ARBITRATION TO PLAINTIFF. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT TREATS PLAINTIFF AS AN EMPLOYEE. THE AGREEMENT IS SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE AND THE REQUIREMENTS OF ARMENDARIZ V. FOUNDATION HEALTH PSYCHARE SERVICES, INC., (2000) 24 CAL.4TH 83, APPLY TO THIS CASE. BOGHOS V. CERTAIN UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYD'S OF LONDON (2005) 36 CAL.4TH 495, DOES NOT CONTROL BECAUSE IT WAS NOT AN EMPLOYMENT CASE AND THE INSTANT PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR INVASION OF PRIVACY IS TETHERED TO THE CALIFORNIA CONSTITUTION'S RIGHT TO PRIVACY. THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT DOES NOT COMPLY WITH ARMENDARIZ BECAUSE: (1) SECTION 17.3 OF THE CPR RULES ALLOWS THE ARBITRATOR TO SHIFT ALL FEES/COSTS TO PLAINTIFF; (2) PARAGRAPH 9.6(C)(V) [NO FORMAL DISCOVERY] IS CRYPTIC AND IT IS UNCLEAR EXACTLY WHAT TYPE OF DISCOVERY PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO. THAT CLAUSE OUGHT TO BE CONSTRUED AGAINST DEFENDANT DRAFTER; AND (3) THE AGREEMENT IMPERMISSIBLY BARS THE RECOVERY OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE COURT DETERMINES THAT SEVERANCE IS NOT APPROPRIATE GIVEN THE NUMBER OF UNCONSCIONABLE PROVISIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

Mtn To Compel Arb And To Stay State Court Proceedings
Off Calendar Jan-31-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RICHARD MUNZINGER OF SHARTSIS FRIESE, TEL. NO. 421-6500 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JEFFREY . LOKEY OF GREENBERG/TRAURIG LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (650) 289-7890 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TUCKER ELLIS, LLP'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay State Court Proceedings - The Court denies the motion to compel arbitration. The agreement is procedurally unconscionable because it was presented to plaintiff in a take-it-or-leave-it manner and defendant failed to provide a copy of the rules that govern the arbitration to plaintiff. The court further notes that the parties' agreement treats plaintiff as an employee. The agreement is substantively unconscionable and the requirements of Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychare Services, Inc., (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83, apply to this case. Boghos v. Certain Underwriters at Lloyd's of London (2005) 36 Cal.4th 495, does not control because it was not an employment case and the instant plaintiff's claim for invasion of privacy is tethered to the California Constitution's right to privacy. The parties' agreement does not comply with Armendariz because: (1) Section 17.3 of the CPR rules allows the arbitrator to shift all fees/costs to plaintiff; (2) Paragraph 9.6(c)(v) [no formal discovery] is cryptic and it is unclear exactly what type of discovery plaintiff is entitled to. That clause ought to be construed against defendant drafter; and (3) the agreement impermissibly bars the recovery of punitive damages. The Court determines that severance is not appropriate given the number of unconscionable provisions. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
